```{r Figure S3a function}
library(readr)
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(matrixStats)
library(data.table)

# function to process a file and return a tidy dataframe for plotting
process_file <- function(filepath, direction_label) {
  df <- as_tibble(fread(here(filepath)))
  
  # define microarray and RNAseq individual signature columns
  microarray_cols <- colnames(df)[2:7]
  rnaseq_cols <- setdiff(colnames(df)[2:(ncol(df) - 2)], microarray_cols)

  # compute median of individual signatures for each Technology
  median_micro <- rowMedians(as.matrix(df[, microarray_cols]), na.rm = TRUE)
  median_rna <- rowMedians(as.matrix(df[, rnaseq_cols]), na.rm = TRUE)

  # compute squared deviation from the median
  sq_dev_micro <- (df$aggregated_microarray - median_micro)^2 / length(microarray_cols)
  sq_dev_rna <- (df$aggregated_RNAseq - median_rna)^2 / length(rnaseq_cols)
  
  # combine into tidy data frame with direction label
  tibble(
    Pathway = df$Description,
    Microarray = sq_dev_micro,
    RNAseq = sq_dev_rna,
    Direction = direction_label
  ) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(Microarray, RNAseq), names_to = "Technology", values_to = "SquaredDeviation")
}
```

```{r Figure S3a plot}
# process both up- and dn-regulated pathways files
plot_df_up <- process_file("figures/figure2/figure2a//marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_pathways.tsv", "Upregulated")
plot_df_dn <- process_file("figures/figure2/figure2b/marray_rnaseq_asinh_dn_pathways.tsv", "Downregulated")

# combine both into one dataframe
combined_plot_df <- bind_rows(plot_df_up, plot_df_dn) %>%
  mutate(Direction = factor(Direction, levels = c("Upregulated", "Downregulated")))

# compute medians for label annotations
med_labels <- combined_plot_df %>%
  group_by(Direction, Technology) %>%
  summarise(SquaredDeviation = median(SquaredDeviation, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# plot
ggplot(combined_plot_df, aes(x = Technology, y = SquaredDeviation, fill = Technology)) +
  #geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #coord_trans(y = "log10") +
  #scale_y_log10(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  geom_text(data = med_labels,
            aes(x = Technology, y = SquaredDeviation, label = format(SquaredDeviation, scientific = TRUE, digits = 4)
),
            vjust = -1,
            hjust = -2,
            size = 5,
            color = "black",
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Squared deviation of aggregated signatures from median of individual signatures",
    y = "Squared deviation",
    x = "Technology"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~Direction) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +   
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(size = 24, hjust = 0.5), 
    axis.title = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 18),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 20)  
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")

```

```{r figure S3b data}
library(dplyr)

# choose how many lowest SD pathways you want to plot per group
top_n <- 10

lowest_sd_paths <- combined_plot_df %>%
  group_by(Direction, Technology) %>%
  slice_min(order_by = SquaredDeviation, n = top_n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

```

```{r}
lowest_sd_paths_all <- combined_plot_df %>%
  group_by(Direction, Technology) %>%
  filter(
    SquaredDeviation < quantile(SquaredDeviation, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


print(dim(combined_plot_df))
print(dim(lowest_sd_paths_all))
print(dim(lowest_sd_paths_all)[1]/dim(combined_plot_df)[1]*100) # percentage of pathways with zero squared deviation
```

```{r}
lowest_sd_paths_microarray <- combined_plot_df %>%
  filter(Technology == "Microarray") %>%
  group_by(Direction) %>%
  filter(
    SquaredDeviation <= quantile(SquaredDeviation, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
print(dim(lowest_sd_paths_microarray)[1]/dim(combined_plot_df[combined_plot_df$Technology == "Microarray",])[1]*100) # percentage of pathways with zero squared deviation

lowest_sd_paths_rnaseq <- combined_plot_df %>%
  filter(Technology == "RNAseq") %>%
  group_by(Direction) %>%
  filter(
    SquaredDeviation <= quantile(SquaredDeviation, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
print(dim(lowest_sd_paths_rnaseq)[1]/dim(combined_plot_df[combined_plot_df$Technology == "RNAseq",])[1]*100) # percentage of pathways with zero squared deviation


```

```{r pathway enrichment matrices}
marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_mat = read_tsv(here("figures/figure2/figure2a/marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_pathways.tsv"))
marray_rnaseq_asinh_dn_mat = read_tsv(here("figures/figure2/figure2b/marray_rnaseq_asinh_dn_pathways.tsv"))

marray_up_not_captured = marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_mat$Description[marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_mat$aggregated_microarray == 0]
rnaseq_up_not_captured = marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_mat$Description[marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_mat$aggregated_RNAseq == 0]

marray_dn_not_captured = marray_rnaseq_asinh_dn_mat$Description[marray_rnaseq_asinh_dn_mat$aggregated_microarray == 0]
rnaseq_dn_not_captured = marray_rnaseq_asinh_dn_mat$Description[marray_rnaseq_asinh_dn_mat$aggregated_RNAseq == 0]
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

dominant_pathways <- marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_mat %>%
  mutate(
    nonzero_count = rowSums(
      across(2:(ncol(.) - 2), ~ . != 0),
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    nonzero_prop = nonzero_count / (ncol(.) - 3)
  ) %>%
  filter(nonzero_prop >= 0.9)

The first 6 are microarray and the rest is RNAseq (exclude "Description" col and aggregated_microarray and aggregated_RNAseq)
I want to make a plot that prove that pathways enriched in 90% of the signatures (microrray and rnaseq separately) tend to have lower "SquaredDeviation" indicated in combined_plot_df
names(combined_plot_df)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# column indices
micro_cols <- 2:7
rnaseq_cols <- 8:(ncol(marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_mat) - 2)


dominant_microarray <- marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_mat %>%
  mutate(
    nonzero_prop = rowMeans(across(all_of(micro_cols), ~ . != 0), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(nonzero_prop >= 0.9) %>%
  pull(1)   # pathway names

dominant_rnaseq <- marray_rnaseq_asinh_up_mat %>%
  mutate(
    nonzero_prop = rowMeans(across(all_of(rnaseq_cols), ~ . != 0), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(nonzero_prop >= 0.9) %>%
  pull(1)

combined_plot_df_up <- combined_plot_df %>%
  filter(Direction == "Upregulated") %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_90 = case_when(
      Technology == "Microarray" & Pathway %in% dominant_microarray ~ "≥90% enriched",
      Technology == "RNAseq"     & Pathway %in% dominant_rnaseq     ~ "≥90% enriched",
      TRUE ~ "<90% enriched"
    )
  )

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  combined_plot_df_up %>% filter(Direction == "Upregulated"),
  aes(x = dominant_90, y = SquaredDeviation)
) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  #geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Technology, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log10") +
  labs(
    x = "Upregulated GO:BP terms prevalence across signatures",
    y = "Squared deviation from aggregated signature",
    title = "Upregulated GO:BP terms consistently enriched across signatures show lower deviation"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)



```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  combined_plot_df_dn %>% filter(Direction == "Downregulated"),
  aes(x = dominant_90, y = SquaredDeviation)
) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  #geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Technology, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log10") +
  labs(
    x = "Downregulated GO:BP terms prevalence across signatures",
    y = "Squared deviation from aggregated signature",
    title = "Downregulated GO:BP consistently enriched \n across signatures show lower deviation"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13)



```

```{r}
micro_cols <- 2:7
rnaseq_cols <- 8:(ncol(marray_rnaseq_asinh_dn_mat) - 2)


dominant_microarray <- marray_rnaseq_asinh_dn_mat %>%
  mutate(
    nonzero_prop = rowMeans(across(all_of(micro_cols), ~ . != 0), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(nonzero_prop >= 0.9) %>%
  pull(1)   # pathway names

dominant_rnaseq <- marray_rnaseq_asinh_dn_mat %>%
  mutate(
    nonzero_prop = rowMeans(across(all_of(rnaseq_cols), ~ . != 0), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(nonzero_prop >= 0.9) %>%
  pull(1)

combined_plot_df_dn <- combined_plot_df %>%
  filter(Direction == "Downregulated") %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_90 = case_when(
      Technology == "Microarray" & Pathway %in% dominant_microarray ~ "≥90% enriched",
      Technology == "RNAseq"     & Pathway %in% dominant_rnaseq     ~ "≥90% enriched",
      TRUE ~ "<90% enriched"
    )
  )

```

```{r combine plots}
combined_plot_df_dom <- combined_plot_df_dom %>%
  mutate(
    Direction = factor(Direction, levels = c("Upregulated", "Downregulated")),
    dominant_90 = factor(dominant_90, levels = c("<90% enriched", "≥90% enriched")),
    Technology = factor(Technology, levels = c("Microarray", "RNAseq"))
  )


library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  combined_plot_df_dom,
  aes(x = Direction, y = SquaredDeviation)
) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.65) +
  facet_grid(
    . ~ Technology + dominant_90,
    scales = "free_y"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log10") +
  labs(
    x = "Regulation direction",
    y = "Squared deviation from aggregated signature",
    title = "Dominant pathways exhibit lower deviation across Up/Down regulation and technologies"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(1.2, "lines")
  )



```


```{r figure S3b; plotting}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(lowest_sd_paths, aes(x = reorder(Pathway, SquaredDeviation), y = SquaredDeviation, fill = Technology)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Direction, scales = "free_x") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Pathways well-captured by aggregated sigatures (lowest squared deviation)",
    x = "Pathway",
    y = "Squared deviation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```

```{r figure S3c data}
# choose how many highest SD pathways you want to plot per group
top_n <- 10

highest_sd_paths <- combined_plot_df %>%
  group_by(Direction, Technology) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = SquaredDeviation, n = top_n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

```

```{r figure S3c; plotting}
library(ggplot2)

#pdf(here("figures/figureS5/FigureS5c_squared_deviation_less_captured.pdf"),
#    width = 16, height = 8)  

ggplot(highest_sd_paths, aes(x = reorder(Pathway, SquaredDeviation), y = SquaredDeviation, fill = Technology)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Direction, scales = "free_x") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Pathways less conserved in aggregated sigatures \n (highest squared deviation)",
    x = "Pathway",
    y = "Squared deviation"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20, hjust = 0.5), 
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 15)
  )

#dev.off()
```
